This stage is a cross-cutting test layer that validates the system’s extensibility surface: how plugins, extensions, skills, MCP servers, and tool definitions are discovered, normalized, exposed, and executed before they participate in the main runtime. The plugin-focused tests cover the full lifecycle: fixture builders in core-plugins and core create realistic homes and configs; provider, loader, store, marketplace, remote, startup-sync, share, manager, discoverable, and app/MCP-routing suites verify path rebinding, filesystem isolation, install/list flows, curated and remote marketplace handling, startup synchronization, capability projection, suggestion logic, and visibility rules. Core plugin adapters then test discoverability, mention parsing, rendering, connector integration, and install prompts.

Skills tests validate explicit and implicit invocation, filesystem scanning, metadata resolution, manager policy, executor-backed discovery, and the skills extension’s prompt/catalog behavior. Extension API and concrete extension suites check registry ordering, state storage, lifecycle hooks, capability defaults, and end-to-end behavior for goal, image-generation, memories, and ad-hoc instruction seeding. MCP tests cover plugin-derived config, executor-backed loading, catalog precedence, connection management, hosted and executor-plugin overlays, and full client transport behavior. Finally, tool tests lock down conversion, serialization, schema policy, discovery filtering, code-mode adaptation, and Responses API shaping.
